provenance: Sampled from the approved external Eldoria farm environment style-lock reference (generated from the approved farm and Waking Gate references). That style-lock image is STYLE REFERENCE ONLY and is intentionally NOT committed to this repository; only these sampled hex values are locked here.

contractFamilyMapping._note: These environment-palette family names map onto the contract's canonical Section 4 sub-palette vocabulary used by the target JSONs' paletteFamilies arrays. The target files keep the contract names; this file locks their actual hex.
contractFamilyMapping.water: arcane (cool water range only; magical violet/glow accents remain the Wildbloom accents below)

wildbloomAccents._source: Authoritative runtime accents from src/presentation/WildbloomDiscoveryController.ts WILDBLOOM_SPOTS. These are gameplay-pinned identity colors, NOT environment base swatches, and must be preserved exactly.

notes: Specification only; no art is included.

This is the concrete hex lock the visual contract Section 4 requires before farm production art is committed, so independently generated assets cannot silently drift on what 'forest', 'wood_leather', water tones, or 'metal_stone' mean. It is versioned (v1): revising a color is an explicit, reviewable change, not silent drift.

Family swatches are ordered darkest-to-lightest. The artist/generator applies the contract's upper-left key light so highlights land upper-left and shadows fall down-right. Do not bake a scene-level warm/gold ambient tint into source art (contract Section 14 applies that once, at the scene level).

The environment base swatches do not replace the Wildbloom identity accents; both coexist. The soft ground shadow under dynamic actors/props (contract Section 8a) is engine-drawn, not a palette color, and is intentionally not listed here.

Scope: farm environment kit only. Character sub-palettes (skin_hair, ui_neutral, ruins, lava) are out of scope and locked separately when their own production art lands. This is not a master-palette redesign.
